name: Riverbank
description_short: RiverBank is a community bank offering business checking, lending, merchant services, and digital banking tools. FDIC-insured with a focus on personalized relationship banking.
description_long:
RiverBank is a regional bank that emphasizes relationship-based business banking combined with modern digital tools. Based on their website and phone number (509), they appear to operate in the Pacific Northwest, specifically serving the Spokane, Washington area. The bank positions itself as an alternative to larger national institutions, highlighting personalized service and local ownership.

RiverBank's core offerings include business checking accounts, business lending (real estate, acquisitions, lines of credit), merchant services with point-of-sale systems, and prepaid payroll solutions through Dash Solutions. They also offer streamlined online business account opening with documentation upload capability. Their merchant services include next-day funding and 24/7 customer support, with equipment ranging from countertop terminals to full-scale POS systems.

The bank differentiates itself through explicit relationship banking—testimonials emphasize that customers are "not just a number." They advertise flexible, customized solutions and claim to understand individual business needs. The bank also highlights courier services, local ownership, and the ability to work with business owners on planning and strategy. FDIC insurance is prominently displayed on their homepage.

However, the website provides limited information about consumer banking products, interest rates, fees, or credit products. There is no pricing transparency, and the site focuses almost exclusively on business customers. The company appears regional rather than national, which could limit accessibility. No information is available about personal loans, credit cards, or savings accounts—suggesting this is a business-focused bank with minimal retail consumer offerings. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Business lending (real estate, acquisitions, operating lines of credit, investment lines of credit)
Secure document upload for account applications
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government
Merchant services with next-day funding and award-winning 24/7 support
Streamlined online business account opening with secure document upload
Customized business lending solutions for various purposes (real estate, acquisitions, lines of credit)
Local, relationship-based banking with named banker support throughout loan process
Prepaid payroll solutions (Dash Solutions) for flexible employee payment methods
Courier service for business customers to ease operations
cons: No pricing, fee, or interest rate information disclosed on website
Appears to be regional/local bank with limited geographic availability (Spokane area)
Minimal consumer banking products—website focuses almost exclusively on business accounts
No credit card, personal loan, or individual savings products mentioned
Limited information about account requirements, minimum balances, or qualification criteria

contact.address: 202 E Spokane Falls Blvd, Spokane, WA 99202

best_for: Small to mid-sized business owners seeking personalized banking relationships
Businesses needing merchant services with customized payment processing plans
Companies in the Spokane, Washington area looking for local bank support
diagnosis: RiverBank is best for business owners in the Pacific Northwest seeking relationship-based commercial banking with customized lending and merchant services. The main caveat is that this is a regional business bank with no apparent consumer banking products, and the website lacks pricing transparency—calling ahead or visiting in person is necessary to understand fees and rates.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 202 E Spokane Falls Blvd, Spokane, WA 99202
meta_description: RiverBank offers business checking, lending, merchant services, and digital banking with FDIC insurance and personalized relationship focus.
